title: 18 Deposit Reports
body_text:
18	Deposit Reports
18.1	Overview of Deposit Reports
Use the reports described in this chapter to confirm that Central 1 received your financial institutionâs paper deposits (Branch Capture or Corporate Capture) and your customersâ mobile deposits.
These reports are available in the MERG file, which can be downloaded from Central 1âs File and Report Exchange. Find MERG files in your financial institutionâs root directory. If applicable, PDFs of these reports are also available in the Clearing folder. Go to the âCADâ subfolder to access Canadian dollar reports and the âUSDâ subfolder to access US dollar reports. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information on accessing the File and Report Exchange.
Acknowledgment reports are available for deposits in Canadian dollars and US dollars.
18.2	ADC â Acknowledgement of Canadian Dollar Deposits
Description
The ADC report lists each deposit sent to Central 1 on the report date. The amount of each deposit (as per the deposit slip and/or virtual deposit slip) is provided on the report. Individual deposit items arenât listed. The following items are included on the ADC report:
Canadian dollar cheques, drafts, and money orders. This includes paper items processed via Branch Capture, Corporate Capture, and Mobile Deposit.
Canada Savings Bonds
Ontario Savings Bonds
CCIN paper remittances
Non-CCIN paper remittances
Dishonoured Canadian dollar items returned via ORS (these items are listed in the ORPI report)
Canadian dollar deposit credit and debit adjustments
Purpose
Financial institutions use the ADC report to verify the amount and date that deposits are credited to their Canadian dollar account at Central 1. Each deposit is posted to your financial institutionâs account separately.
Organization
Each deposit appears on one line.
Deposits are sorted by branch.
Mobile deposits are listed separately from paper deposits.
Regardless of which branch the depositing customer is associated with, all mobile deposits are credited to a single transit number as specified by your financial institution. See Figure 1.
Distribution
The report is available to your financial institutionâs head office the day after the deposit is processed at Central 1.
Figure 1: ADC Report
18.3	ADUS â Acknowledgement of US Dollar Deposits
Description
The ADUS report lists each deposit sent to Central 1 on the report date. The amount of each deposit (as per the deposit slip) is provided on the report. Individual deposit items arenât listed. The following items are included on the ADUS report:
US dollar cheques, drafts, and money orders. This includes paper items processed via Branch Capture and Corporate Capture.
Dishonoured US dollar items returned via ORS (these items are listed in the ORUI report)
US dollar deposit credit and debit adjustments
Purpose
Financial institutions use the ADUS report to verify the amount and date that deposits are credited to their US dollar account at Central 1. Each deposit is posted individually.
Organization
Each deposit appears on one line. Deposits are sorted by branch. See Figure 2.
Distribution
The report is available to your financial institutionâs head office the day after the deposit is processed at Central 1.
Figure 2: ADUS Report
18.4	RDLC â Remote Deposit System, Mobile Remote Deposits
Description
The RDLC report lists individual Mobile Deposit transactions.
Purpose
Financial institutions use this report to view Mobile Deposit transactions or to manually credit customersâ accounts (if the accounts arenât credited in real-time or if the deposit file isnât used to facilitate automatic posting).
Organization
Each Mobile Deposit transaction is listed on one line.
The report includes all Mobile Deposit transactions (i.e., the report doesnât separate transactions by deposit slip).
The item count and dollar value of all mobile deposits are provided on the report.
Regardless of which branch the customer is associated with, all mobile deposits are credited to a single transit number as specified by your financial institution. See Figure 3.
Distribution
The report is available to your financial institutionâs head office the day after the deposit is processed at Central 1.
Figure 3: RDLC Report
18.5	RDBC â Remote Deposit System Branch Capture Deposits
Description
The RDBC report lists individual Branch Capture and Corporate Capture Canadian dollar items. It also contains a summary and detail information for deposits processed in Branch Capture and Corporate Capture. If a financial institution has only implemented Branch Capture, the report will only list Branch Capture transactions. If a financial institution has implemented Branch Capture and Corporate Capture, the report will list both Branch Capture and Corporate Capture transactions.
This report includes Canadian Savings Bonds (Branch Capture only). Financial institution transactions are posted per transit number and/or unlisted transit number. See Figure 4 and Figure 5.
Note: 	Financial institutions that have implemented Corporate Capture can identify their business customerâs unlisted transit number in the âDEPOSITED TOâ section, where the branch number is preceded by âCC.â See Figure 5.
Figure 4: RDBC Report â Branch Capture
Figure 5: RDBC Report â Corporate Capture
Purpose
Financial institutions use this report as a supplementary report to the ADC report, which is used for reconciliation. The RDBC report consists of a detailed listing and summary of deposits transmitted to Central 1 by the Branch Capture and Corporate Capture deadline (7 pm local time each business day). The totals in the report donât reflect any adjustments made.
Compare this report with the reports generated on the Branch Capture and/or Corporate Capture application to determine which deposits were transmitted to Central 1 by the Branch Capture and/or Corporate Capture deadline and to identify which deposits are processed the next business day.
Organization
The Deposit Detail section contains the information of each item included in the deposit, such as:
Itemâs sequence in the deposit
Date it was processed
Deposit trace ID
Transit number
Account number
Amount of the item
The Summary section lists the deposits and includes:
Deposit ID number of each deposit
Deposit trace ID
Item count in the deposit
Total amount of the deposit
Date the deposit was captured and accepted
Distribution
The report is available to your financial institutionâs head office the day after the deposit is processed at Central 1.
18.6	RDBU â Remote Deposit System Branch Capture and Corporate Capture Deposits
Description
The RDBU report lists individual Branch Capture and Corporate Capture US dollar items. It also contains a summary and detail information for deposits processed in Branch Capture and Corporate Capture. If a financial institution has only implemented Branch Capture, the report will only list Branch Capture transactions. If a financial institution has implemented Branch Capture and Corporate Capture, the report will list both Branch Capture and Corporate Capture transactions.
Financial institution transactions are posted per transit number and/or unlisted transit number. See Figure 6 and Figure 7.
Note:
Financial institutions that have implemented Corporate Capture can identify their business customerâs unlisted transit number in the âDEPOSITED TOâ section, where the branch number is preceded by âCC.â See Figure 7.
Figure 6: RDBU Report â Branch Capture
Figure 7: RDBU Report â Corporate Capture
Purpose
Financial institutions use this report as a supplementary report to the ADUS report, which is used for reconciliation. The RDBU report consists of a detailed listing and summary of deposits submitted to Central 1 by the Branch Capture and Corporate Capture deadline (7 pm local time each business day). The totals in the report donât reflect any adjustments made.
Compare this report with the reports generated on the Branch Capture and Corporate Capture application(s), if applicable, to determine which deposits were transmitted to Central 1 by the Branch Capture and Corporate Capture deadline and to identify which deposits are processed the next business day.
Organization
The Deposit Detail section contains the information of each item included in the deposit, such as:
Itemâs sequence in the deposit
Date it was processed
Deposit trace ID
Transit number
Account number
Amount of the item
The Summary section lists the deposits and includes:
Deposit ID number of each deposit
Deposit trace ID
Item count in the deposit
Total amount of the deposit
Date the deposit was captured and accepted
Distribution
The report is available to your financial institutionâs head office the day after the deposit is processed at Central 1.
18.7	BL01 â Scanner Count for Billing â Branch
Description
The BL01 report provides Branch Capture scanner count information for all active scanners at your financial institution during the business month.
Scanner billing references the scanner serial number thatâs recorded when deposit items are scanned and transmitted for processing. An active scanner is defined as at least one deposit scanned and transmitted during the business month.
Figure 8: BL01 Report â Scanner Count for Branch Capture
Purpose
Financial institutions use this report to reconcile Branch Capture monthly scanner billing.
Organization
The report detail contains a list of active scanner serial numbers and the related scanner model. The summary section contains the scanner count. All scanners are reported under your head office transit number.
Distribution
The report is available to your financial institutionâs head office the first business day of the following month.
18.8	RL01 â Scanner Count for Billing â Corporate
Description
The RL01 report provides Corporate Capture scanner count information for all active scanners at your business customerâs locations during the business month.
Scanner billing references the scanner serial number thatâs recorded when deposit items are scanned and transmitted for processing. An active scanner is defined as at least one deposit scanned and transmitted during the business month.
Figure 9: RL01 Report â Scanner Count for Corporate Capture
Purpose
Financial institutions use this report to reconcile Corporate Capture monthly scanner billing. Optionally, the report can assist with billing business customers for scanner fees.
Organization
The report detail contains the business name, a list of active scanner serial numbers, the related scanner model, and the scanner count for each business location (unlisted transit). The summary section contains the scanner count for all businesses at your financial institution. All scanners are reported under your head office transit number.
Distribution
The report is available to your financial institutionâs head office the first business day of the following month.
18.9 	Branch Capture Reports
Branch Capture users can generate reports on the Branch Capture application. However, the reports that can be generated are limited by user type (e.g., Capture User, Reviewer, and Supervisor) and the location (i.e., transit number) to which the individual is granted accessed. See Chapter 13, Branch Capture Reports and Research, for procedures.
The table below lists the Branch Capture user types, reports, and the user access.
User Type
Reports
User Access
Capture User
Deposit Item Detail
Deposit List Report
Reports are generated via the Deposits function. Access to locations (i.e., transit numbers) are controlled by user permissions.
Reviewer
Deposit Item Detail
Deposit List Report
Reports are generated via the Deposits function. Access to locations (i.e., transit numbers) are controlled by user permissions.
Supervisor
Deposit Item Detail
Deposit List Report
Deposit Details
Deposit Details by Deposit Number
Deposit Summary
Item Research (Query)
Reports are generated from the Deposits, Reports, and Research functions. Access to locations (i.e., transit numbers) are controlled by user permissions.
Administrator
Deposit Details
Deposit Details by Deposit Number
Deposit Summary
Item Research (Query)
Reports are generated from the Deposits, Reports, and Research functions. Administrators can generate reports for all of your locations.
18.10 	Corporate Capture Reports
Corporate Capture users can generate reports on the Corporate Capture application. However, the reports that can be generated are limited by the user type (i.e., Capture User, Supervisor, Analyst) and the location (i.e., the unlisted transit number) to which the individual is granted access. For procedures on generating the Corporate Capture reports available to each type of user, see the applicable Corporate Capture user guide (e.g., for Capture User, for Supervisor, for Analyst).
The table below lists the Corporate Capture user types, reports, and the user access.
User Type
Reports
User Access
Capture User
Deposit Item Detail
Deposit List Report
Reports are generated via the Deposits function. Access to locations (i.e., transit numbers) are controlled by user permissions.
Supervisor
Deposit Summary
Image Deposit Item Detail
Deposit Details
Deposit Details by Deposit Number
Deposit Summary
Item Research (Query)
Reports are generated via the Deposits function. Access to locations (i.e., transit numbers) are controlled by user permissions.
Analyst
Deposit Details
Deposit Details by Deposit Number
Deposit Summary
Item Research (Query)
Reports are generated from the Deposits, Reports, and Research tabs. Access to locations (i.e., transit numbers) are controlled by user permissions.
